[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: _updatePosition] Can an unprivileged lender, by having their position slashed (lossFactor increases) and then calling updatePosition, cause the accrual formula to use postSlashPendingFee (reduced by slash) but the denominator (maturity - lastAccrual) remains unchanged, resulting in a smaller accrued fee than if the slash had not occurred, underclaiming continuous fee from the slashed lender? Preconditions: lender with credit=C, pendingFee=P, bad debt occurs increasing lossFactor. Call sequence: createBadDebt -> updatePosition(lender). Invariant tested: accrued fee after slash == postSlashPendingFee * elapsed / (maturity - lastAccrual). Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed from slashed lender; protocol receives less fee after bad debt. Proof idea: unit test comparing accrued fee before and after slash, assert fee proportional to postSlashPendingFee.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: claimContinuousFee] Can an unprivileged fee claimer, by calling claimContinuousFee(market, amount, receiver) where amount == continuousFeeCredit but the market has multiple lenders with unclaimed pendingFee, cause the claimed amount to exceed the tokens that will actually be collected from lenders (since pendingFee is only crystallized on _updatePosition), making the continuous fee overclaimed relative to future crystallizable fees? Preconditions: multiple lenders with pendingFee > 0 not yet crystallized, continuousFeeCredit < sum(pendingFee). Call sequence: claimContinuousFee(market, continuousFeeCredit, receiver) before any updatePosition calls. Invariant tested: continuousFeeCredit <= tokens already collected from lenders (not future pendingFee). Scoped impact: continuous fee correctly bounded by continuousFeeCredit; verify no overclaim of future fees. Proof idea: assert claimContinuousFee(amount) where amount <= continuousFeeCredit always succeeds and does not claim future pendingFee.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, by taking a sell offer (offer.buy=false) with tick=0 (price=0) and nonzero settlementFee, cause buyerPrice = 0 + settlementFee > 0 and sellerPrice = 0, so buyerAssets = units.mulDivUp(settlementFee, WAD) > 0 and sellerAssets = units.mulDivUp(0, WAD) = 0, resulting in the entire buyerAssets going to claimableSettlementFee with the seller receiving nothing, charging the full settlement fee to the buyer with zero proceeds to the seller? Invariant tested: sellerAssets >= 0 and claimableSettlementFee increases by buyerAssets - sellerAssets = buyerAssets. Scoped impact: settlement fee charged correctly but seller receives zero; verify no accounting error. Proof idea: unit test with tick=0, fee>0, assert claimableSettlementFee == buyerAssets and sellerAssets == 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, by taking a buy offer (offer.buy=true) where offerPrice < settlementFee (causing sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee to underflow), trigger an arithmetic underflow revert, effectively making the offer untakeable and freezing the maker's offer capacity, rather than the settlement fee being capped or the offer being gracefully rejected? Call sequence: setMarketSettlementFee(id,1,high_fee) -> take(buyOffer, units) where offerPrice < settlementFee. Invariant tested: take reverts cleanly (not silently miscalculates) when offerPrice < settlementFee for buy offers. [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, by taking a sell offer (offer.buy=false) with maxAssets tracking sellerAssets, cause the settlement fee to be effectively borne by the buyer (who pays buyerAssets > sellerAssets) while the seller's consumed tracking uses sellerAssets, meaning the settlement fee is charged to the wrong side (buyer pays extra, seller's capacity is not reduced by the fee), violating the invariant that the settlement fee spread is symmetric? Scoped impact: settlement fee correctly charged to buyer; verify consumed tracking does not allow seller to overfill. Proof idea: fuzz units, settlementFee; assert consumed[seller][group] == sum(sellerAssets) and buyer pays sum(buyerAssets).

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, by taking a buy offer (offer.buy=true) with maxAssets tracking buyerAssets (which includes settlement fee), cause the maker's consumed capacity to be reduced by buyerAssets (including fee), so the maker effectively has less capacity than intended because the settlement fee eats into their maxAssets budget, charging the settlement fee to the wrong side (maker's capacity reduced by fee they don't control)? Scoped impact: settlement fee reduces maker's effective offer capacity; maker's intent is violated. Proof idea: fuzz units, settlementFee, maxAssets; assert total units filled == maxAssets * WAD / buyerPrice within rounding.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: take / _updatePosition] Can an unprivileged taker, by triggering a take() that calls _updatePosition for the buyer (who has no credit, hasCredit=false) only when units > buyerPos.debt, cause the buyer's accrued fee to be zero (since credit=0 before the take), but after the take the buyer has new credit with pendingFee set to buyerPendingFeeIncrease, verifying that the continuous fee is correctly initialized for new lenders and not underclaimed? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, by taking a sell offer (offer.buy=false) with units such that sellerAssets = units.mulDivUp(sellerPrice, WAD) > buyerAssets = units.mulDivUp(buyerPrice, WAD) (impossible by construction since buyerPrice >= sellerPrice), or by finding a rounding edge case where buyerAssets < sellerAssets, cause claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] += buyerAssets - sellerAssets to underflow, reverting the take and making the offer untakeable? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: take / claimContinuousFee] Can an unprivileged lender, by withdrawing all credit after continuous fee accrual (pendingFee fully crystallized into continuousFeeCredit), cause the fee claimer to call claimContinuousFee(market, continuousFeeCredit, receiver) and successfully claim the fee, but then the lender's credit is 0 and withdrawable is also 0 (since withdraw decremented it), making the continuous fee claim drain the last withdrawable tokens that were supposed to cover the lender's credit, causing the lender to receive fewer tokens than their credit? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, by taking a buy offer (offer.buy=true) in a market where the continuous fee was changed by feeSetter between two takes, cause the second take to use the new continuousFee for buyerPendingFeeIncrease calculation while the first lender's existing pendingFee was set at the old rate, resulting in the total pendingFee across all lenders being inconsistent with the market's current continuousFee, causing the continuous fee to be overclaimed or underclaimed relative to the blended rate?
